preview_text:
The model backs Turkey as the marginal favorite at 36%, but the gap over Australia sits at less than 4 percentage points. Australia's win probability stands at 32%, leaving the match genuinely competitive from the statistical perspective. Turkey holds a modest edge, yet the underdog has a realistic path to victory.

The draw probability of 32% underscores how tightly matched these sides appear in the model's view. A third of all outcomes end level, and the win probabilities for each team are separated by only 4 points. This is a congested forecast, suggesting a low-scoring or closely contested match where either result remains plausible.

The market consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks and Polymarket traders price Turkey substantially higher at 56–57%, a gap of roughly 20 percentage points above the model's 36%. Conversely, they assign Australia only 17–18%, well below the model's 32%. The draw sits closer between sources at 25–26%. The dashboard offers no judgment on which source better predicts the outcome; the divergence warrants awareness.
